title: Texas Hold'em Poker
tagline: Strategy, position, and psychology collide over thousands of hands.
overview: Texas Hold'em is the world's most popular poker variant. Each player receives two private hole cards, and five community cards are revealed in stages: the Flop (3 cards), Turn (1 card), and River (1 card). Players may bet, raise, call, or fold at each stage. The player making the best five-card hand from any combination of hole cards and community cards wins the pot.
rules: Each player is dealt two private hole cards face-down.
Three community cards are dealt face-up (the Flop), then one more (the Turn), then a final card (the River).
Betting occurs after the hole cards, after the Flop, Turn, and River.
Players can fold (forfeit), call (match), raise (increase), or check (pass) depending on the betting action.
At showdown, the best five-card hand using any combination of hole and community cards wins.
Betting order rotates clockwise; position (acting last) is a major strategic advantage.
monteCarloExplanation: The simulation pits different player archetypes head-to-head over many hands. Since no randomisation is removed, the results reflect genuine long-run profitability of each style — including blind steals, position plays, and variance. Key metrics like VPIP (how often a player voluntarily puts money in the pot) and PFR (pre-flop raise %) reveal how tight or loose each strategy is.

strategies.name: Calling Station
strategies.description: Rarely folds or raises — calls almost every bet. Predictable and exploitable.

strategies.name: Tight Passive
strategies.description: Plays few hands but rarely bets aggressively. Loses value on strong hands.

strategies.name: Tight Aggressive (TAG)
strategies.description: Plays few hands, bets aggressively when in. The most profitable style long-term.

strategies.name: Loose Aggressive (LAG)
strategies.description: Plays many hands aggressively. High variance but profitable against weaker players.

strategies.name: Pot Odds Player
strategies.description: Makes decisions based on mathematical pot odds and implied odds.

strategies.name: Bluffer
strategies.description: Frequently bluffs and semi-bluffs — works until opponents adjust.

strategies.name: Position Aware
strategies.description: Tightens from early position and widens from late position.

strategies.name: GTO Balanced
strategies.description: Game Theory Optimal — mathematically unexploitable mixed strategy.
